preamble: Monophthongization of 'ai' to a high vowel 'i' typically involves the assimilation of the first element to the second, or a gradual raising and fronting of the diphthongal nucleus.

examples.note: Various historical developments where diphthongs simplify to high vowels, sometimes as a reverse of stages seen in the Great Vowel Shift.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
